title: Sunday, July 8
context:
# Temporal Context Snapshot: Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
## Sermon Date: July 8, 2018 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon falls on the Seventh Sunday after Pentecost (Proper 10 in the Revised Common Lectionary). The appointed readings for this date would likely include 2 Samuel 5:1-5, 12 (David anointed king), Psalm 23, Ephesians 1:3-14, and Mark 6:14-29 (the death of John the Baptist). The church was in Ordinary Time, the long pastoral season of the Christian year stretching from Pentecost toward Advent.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church in July 2018 was in the immediate aftermath of General Convention held in Austin, Texas (June 28-July 8, with some resolutions voted online afterward). Key actions included reaffirming marriage equality, addressing gun violence through church policy, and continuing work on racial reconciliation. The Diocese of Colorado would have been integrating these national resolutions into local parish life. Bishop Robert O'Neill led the Diocese of Colorado during this period and was actively engaged in the convention.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver in early July 2018 was experiencing typical summer heat and drought conditions affecting Colorado agriculture. The Colorado state legislature had concluded its 2018 session in May. Downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill area were in the midst of summer tourism season. No major natural disasters or immediate local crises marked this particular week, though water availability remained an ongoing concern for the region.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Trump was preparing for a planned summit with Russian President Vladimir Putin scheduled for July 16 in Helsinki, prompting significant controversy and debate about election interference. The U.S. was applying tariffs against China and allies, escalating trade tensions. The separation of migrant families at the U.S.-Mexico border remained a major national story following executive order reversal in late June. The midterm election cycle was intensifying with early primaries and campaigning underway for November elections.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The FIFA World Cup was concluding in Russia (final on July 15), with significant global attention on the tournament. Thailand was managing the cave rescue operation that had concluded successfully on July 10 (just two days after this sermon), with international media focused on the dramatic extraction of the youth soccer team. Trade tensions between the U.S. and Europe, China, and other allies were escalating as the Trump administration imposed tariffs.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in July 2018 reflected deep political polarization, with midterm elections looming and concerns about democratic norms heightened by Russia-related investigations. The #MeToo movement continued shaping conversations about power and accountability in institutions. Summer blockbuster season was active (Black Panther, Incredibles 2, Ant-Man and the Wasp were recent releases). There was a sense of national anxiety mixed with summer leisure, and religious institutions were increasingly called upon to address political and social divisions.
